---
title: "LRP85: Do older-and-more-able children progress more? (growth curves with an age Ã— ability interaction)"

format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
    number-sections: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-links:
      - repo
---

{{< include _partials/_header.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_setup.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_gate_badge.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_key_findings.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_reading_guide.qmd >}}

## Overview

LRP85 is the dedicated growth-curve vehicle for the gain-factor finding (#228 item 10) that the **age Ã— ability interaction** is positive in 6 of 8 outcomes â€” very strong for basic concepts (P = 0.99) and grammar (P = 1.00): _older-and-more-able children progress more than age and ability predict separately_. It extends the independent-core [LRP69](../lrp-rli-gc-069/) by adding a child-level **baseline (t1) age** moderator, and its interaction with baseline non-verbal ability, to each measure's growth **rate**.

The estimand, framing and caveats are otherwise identical to LRP69: a joint multivariate latent growth-curve model on the logit scale, GA-confounded and **descriptive, never causal**.

_Adjusted association, not causal._ Every coefficient is an **adjusted, latent-general-ability-confounded association**. Block design is an off-DAG ability proxy and baseline age is not randomised; the child random intercept only _partially_ adjusts (`dag/dag-language-reading.dagitty`, `METHODS.md`). Read $\gamma^{\text{int}}_m$ / $\gamma^{\text{age}}_m$ / $\gamma_m$ as adjusted associations, never "age or ability drives growth". Exploratory at nâ‰ˆ54 â€” intervals are wide; read the _direction_ of $\gamma^{\text{int}}_m$. Note the growth family models the five verbal/reading trajectories (R/E/T/W/L); concepts (F) and blending (B), where the gain factors' age Ã— ability signal was strongest, are **not** growth-curve outcomes, so this tests the interaction only on those five and cannot directly check the F/B signals â€” expect a mostly-positive pattern echoing the gain factors' 6-of-8 direction.

## Model structure

For each measure $m \in \{R, E, T, W, L\}$, child $i$ and wave $t$, a latent logit level follows a linear growth curve in standardised age $a$: $\theta_m[i,t] = \text{intercept}_m[i] + \text{slope}_m[i]\,a[i,t]$. The intercept is as LRP69 ($\alpha_m + \delta_m z(\text{blocks}_i) + \sigma^0_m z^0_m[i]$); the **slope** gains the age Ã— ability layer:

$$
\text{slope}_m[i] = \beta_m
  + \gamma_m\,z(\text{blocks}_i)
  + \gamma^{\text{age}}_m\,\text{age0}_i
  + \gamma^{\text{int}}_m\,\big(\text{age0}_i \cdot z(\text{blocks}_i)\big)
  + \sigma^1_m z^1_m[i],
$$

where $\text{age0}_i$ is the child's **first-wave age standardised across children** â€” a between-child moderator, distinct from the within-child time axis $a[i,t]$ that the slope multiplies. $\gamma^{\text{int}}_m$ is the headline: positive = a child who is both older at baseline and more able grows faster on measure $m$ than the two main effects predict. $\gamma_m$ (ability â†’ rate, the LRP69 estimand) and $\gamma^{\text{age}}_m$ (baseline age â†’ rate) are the main effects that make the interaction interpretable. Observed counts enter a **masked** Beta-Binomial so a child missing one score still contributes its other waves; the random intercept and slope are independent per measure; the intervention-dose covariate is omitted (revised-DAG `IS` collider); everything is non-centred. Independent-core (no shared growth-tempo factor).

_Symbol mapping._

| Construct | Symbol | Measure | Denominator |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Receptive vocabulary | **R** | `rowpvt` | 170 |
| Expressive vocabulary | **E** | `eowpvt` | 170 |
| Receptive grammar | **T** | `trog` | 32 |
| Word reading | **W** | `ewrswr` | 79 |
| Letter-sound knowledge | **L** | `yarclet` | 32 |

Non-verbal ability is **block design** (`blocks`, WPPSI-III Block Design, t1-only, complete for all 54 children); baseline age is the child's age at t1.

{{< include _partials/_priors.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_prior_predictive.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_results_growth.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_technical.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_footer.qmd >}}
